title: Camps * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Camps * Native American Community 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community AIDS/HIV Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Families/Friends of People With Cancer Former Foster Children Foster Children Muscular Dystrophy Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CAMPS AND SEASONAL PROGRAMMING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers academic and cultural camps during summer and spring break, focusing on math, science, and Native culture. The Ninth Grade Counts camp helps students understand high school graduation requirements, explore college and career options, and learn about Native American art, teachings, and science. Students also meet college students, staff, and Native professionals to learn about different careers. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Grades 2nd-8th, and who self-identified as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CAMPS AND SEASONAL PROGRAMMING
